commit.message:
Run real vLLM instances in disaggregated serving demo

Launch two vLLM servers (Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct) as Prefill and Decode,
route requests through the proxy, and send test prompts end-to-end.
Demonstrates the full Mooncake proxy pattern with real LLM inference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add vLLM disaggregated serving proxy for real Mooncake + vLLM deployment

Implements a simplified version of Mooncake's vllm_v1_proxy_server.py that
routes requests between vLLM Prefill (kv_producer) and Decode (kv_consumer)
instances. Supports round-robin load balancing across multiple nodes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: nano-mooncake — Mooncake minimal prototype in ~650 lines of Python

Implements the core design of Mooncake (FAST 2025 Best Paper) as a teaching prototype:
- Transfer Engine with pluggable TCP transport and batch async transfers
- Mooncake Store with Master/Client, two-phase put, replica tracking, and lease eviction
- HTTP metadata server for segment discovery
- Two runnable examples: Prefill/Decode disaggregation and distributed KVCache store

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat: add recovery key mechanism for vault password reset (#59) (#74)

* feat: add recovery key mechanism for vault password reset (#59)

Refactor key model to use a random master key wrapped by password-derived
KEK (key encryption key), enabling recovery key support:

- Generate 256-bit recovery key encoded as 24 BIP39 mnemonic words
- Master key is now randomly generated and wrapped (encrypted) with both
  the password-derived KEK and a recovery-derived KEK
- Recovery key displayed at vault creation; can be re-displayed later
- Password reset via recovery key: decode words, unwrap master key,
  re-wrap with new password-derived KEK
- Backward compatible: legacy vaults (without wrapped_master_key) still
  work using the old Argon2id-direct model
- CLI commands: recovery-key show, recovery-key reset
- FFI bindings: axiom_vault_get_recovery_key, axiom_vault_reset_password

New files:
- core/crypto/src/recovery.rs - RecoveryKey type with BIP39 encoding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR #74 review — eliminate double KDF, return recovery words

Performance fixes (review blockers 1-4, 10):
- recover_vault: reuse master_key from recovery instead of re-deriving
  via verify_password (saves ~1s Argon2id round)
- change_password: use master_key returned from verify_password instead
  of calling self.master_key() separately
- migrate_to_v1_1: reuse verify_password result as both master key and
  KEK in legacy mode (they're the same value), eliminating second KDF
- create_vault: pass master_key through VaultConfigCreation to avoid
  VaultSession::unlock re-running verify_password
- reset_password_with_recovery: get master_key from recovery key before
  resetting, avoiding post-reset verify_password

API fix (review blocker 5):
- Tauri create_vault now returns VaultCreationResult with recovery_words
  instead of logging them and discarding

UX improvements (review suggestions 6, 11):
- Extract display_recovery_words() helper with confirmation prompt
- Replace 4 duplicated recovery word display blocks with helper call

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix SWC parse error: use 'as' cast instead of useState generics

SWC misparses useState<string[]> in TSX because lowercase 'string'
looks like a JSX tag. Use 'as' type assertion to avoid the ambiguity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: join assemblies in get_stage_daily_stats and get_summary

Orphaned progress entries (assembly_mark not in assemblies table)
were inflating B&P and other stage totals. Adding the same JOIN
used by search_progress makes all metrics consistent.

commit.message:
Replace relURL with site.Home.RelPermalink for correct base path

relURL does not prepend the site's base path — it only strips hostnames
from absolute URLs. Use site.Home.RelPermalink (e.g. /homelab/) to
construct all internal paths correctly regardless of deployment subpath.

commit.message:
docs: READMEのサンプルファイル名から個人名を除去

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Progressive continuity editor — tracks facts across chapters

Replaces the old light review (300 chars of previous chapters) with a
real continuity system. After each chapter is written, extracts a
structured facts sheet (characters, items, injuries, timeline, plot
state). When checking chapter N, the editor gets the accumulated
ledger from chapters 1 through N-1 and catches contradictions like
lost items reappearing, changed physical descriptions, dead characters
coming back, and timeline errors. Facts also feed into the chapter
writing prompt so Claude knows the full story state, not just the
last 500 chars of the previous chapter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix GRDB migration crash on databases created with legacy SQLite C API

Skip migration bodies when the legacy PRAGMA user_version indicates
they were already applied before the GRDB transition.

also preserved across quote refresh cycles.

Actions workflow for auto-generating draft PRs with rule patches.

detection, rolling baselines, threshold-triggered LLM analysis, and
GitHub Actions remediation workflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(drift): add core drift metrics, evaluator, baseline, store, collector, and severity

New package ai-observer/internal/drift/ with:
- KL divergence, PSI, and cross-matrix deviation metrics (pure math)
- DriftSignal/Baseline/Thresholds types
- Evaluator comparing current vs baseline distributions
- Distribution builders for category, confidence, and cross-matrix
- ES mapping and store for drift_baselines index
- Collector for querying classified content
- Severity calculation from drift signals

commit.message:
fix: close compliant crate MEDIUM findings (#648)

## Summary

- **taxis**: Add `snafu::Location` tracking to `ValidationError` with
implicit capture, replacing direct struct construction with
`ValidationSnafu` builder
- **taxis**: Add `#[must_use]` to `requires_restart`,
`restart_prefixes`, and `redact` functions
- **pylon**: Add `Send + Sync` static assertion for `AppState`
- **nous**: Add `# Cancel safety` doc sections to all public async
functions in `handle.rs`, `manager.rs`, and `cross.rs`

Closes #570, Closes #566

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo check -p aletheia-taxis -p aletheia-pylon -p aletheia-nous`
- [x] `cargo test -p aletheia-taxis -p aletheia-pylon -p aletheia-nous`
— all 61 tests pass
- [x] `cargo clippy -p aletheia-taxis -p aletheia-pylon -p aletheia-nous
--all-targets -- -D warnings` — zero warnings
- [x] `cargo fmt -- --check` — clean

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Cody Kickertz <cody@forkwright.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace Mutex .expect() with snafu error propagation (#647)

## Summary

- All `.lock().expect()` and `.read().expect()` calls on Mutex/RwLock
across nous, hermeneus, organon, and integration-tests now carry
`#[expect(clippy::expect_used, reason = "...")]` with documented
justification
- Library code (hermeneus `ProviderHealthTracker`): Option B applied —
methods don't return `Result`, converting would cascade across hermeneus
and nous callers. Short critical sections where poisoning indicates a
prior thread panic
- Test code (nous, organon, integration-tests): All in `#[cfg(test)]`
modules — poisoned locks mean test bugs, panicking is appropriate
- Pylon had zero instances (already clean)

Closes #559

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo clippy -p aletheia-nous -p aletheia-hermeneus -p
aletheia-organon --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean
- [x] `cargo test -p aletheia-nous -p aletheia-hermeneus` — all pass
- [x] `cargo test -p aletheia-organon` — all pass (4 pre-existing
filesystem test failures unrelated to this change)
- [x] `cargo fmt -- --check` — clean
- [x] Verified no remaining undocumented `.expect()` on lock calls

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Cody Kickertz <cody@forkwright.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
UI polish: fix mutuals rendering, create trip button, customize placement (#75)

* Polish itinerary UI: smaller travel/accommodation cards, consistent location style, default to °F

- Remove location from member travel line items (shown in detail sheet instead)
- Make member travel and accommodation line items smaller (text-xs, reduced padding/icons)
- Style location link in member travel detail sheet to match event detail sheet (no underline)
- Default temperature unit to Fahrenheit across all fallback locations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix mutuals grid not rendering due to useScrollReveal race condition

useScrollReveal used useRef which doesn't trigger re-renders. When the
mutuals grid is conditionally rendered (hidden while loading), the ref
is null on first effect run and the IntersectionObserver is never created.
Switch to a callback ref so the observer is set up when the element
appears in the DOM.

Also add loading skeleton to invite dialog mutual suggestions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move create trip button from FAB to header action bar

Replace the floating action button with an inline outline button next
to the "My Trips" title for better discoverability.

* Move customize button to bottom-right of cover photo

Repositioned from top-right overlay to bottom-right, aligned with the
title row for a cleaner layout.

* Fix CI: update E2E tests for renamed button and removed location from travel card

The create trip button was renamed from "Create new trip" to "New Trip" and
the location link was moved from the travel line item to the detail sheet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix CI: restore location display in travel line items

The UI polish commit accidentally removed the location text and Google Maps
link from MemberTravelLineItem, causing E2E tests to fail when looking for
airport names in travel cards.

commit.message:
Fix BML response field mapping: use id and url from transaction

- transaction.id is the BML transaction ID (not transactionId)
- transaction.url is the payment page (not redirectUrl which is our callback)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Comprehensive codebase audit: performance, security, and refactoring

iOS:
- Add task cancellation on view disappear (JobDetailView)
- Cache time-bucketed job lists to avoid re-filtering on every render
- Debounce financial cache updates (100ms) to coalesce rapid changes
- Move image decoding off main thread in PhotoDetailView
- Fix photo loading race condition with .task(id:)
- Add memory warning handler to ImageCacheService
- Replace silent try? with logged do/catch for inventory and activity log ops
- Add input validation to ProKitchen XML importer
- Sanitize Postgres constraint errors in user-facing messages
- Add photo upload failure feedback in share extension
- Add 15s network timeout for image fetching

Web:
- Add staleTime/gcTime to all ~60 React Query hooks (5min stable, 2min volatile)
- Memoize bomProductIds, inventoryByProductId, pick list filters in MaterialsTab
- Fix useUserNames query key (array → sorted string for stable cache)
- Add React.memo to all 9 tab components
- Dynamic imports for tab components (code splitting)
- Remove unused date-fns dependency (-27KB)
- Export all 28 hooks from barrel index
- Fix pick list timestamp to capture on dialog open
- Remove as-any type casts in bank components
- Add print window popup blocker feedback
- Tighten auth callback host validation (strict VERCEL_URL check)
- Consolidate redundant job queries in useReports (5→3 DB queries)

Edge Functions:
- Create shared modules: auth, validation, errors, docusign, quickbooks, plaid
- Refactor 18 functions to use shared auth (~20 lines removed per function)
- Extract duplicated DocuSign JWT generation from 5 functions
- Extract duplicated QuickBooks token refresh from 2 functions
- Extract duplicated Plaid config from 3 functions
- Add input validation (amounts, discounts, string lengths)
- Fix SQL injection in QuickBooks customer query
- Sanitize error messages (no raw errors to clients)
- Add CRITICAL logging for webhook DB failures
- Remove all Deno.env.get()! assertions with safe runtime validation
- Add structured error codes for QB token refresh failures

Net: 879 additions, 2052 deletions (-1173 lines)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: artifact sync — semver tag filtering, git tag fallback, details output

Three bugs fixed:
1. Quay.io/GHCR returned 'main' as first non-'latest' tag. Now filters
   for semver-only tags, preferring 3-part (X.Y.Z) over 2-part (X.Y).
2. GitHub releases 404 on repos with only git tags. Falls back to latest
   semver git tag via /tags endpoint.
3. GHCR excluded from comparison when it has no semver tags (many repos
   only push 'latest'+'main' to GHCR).

Artifact sync now returns detail string showing per-source versions.

Result: 5/9 MCP repos now pass. 4 failures are real drift (stale PyPI
or untagged releases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
